steps.title: Welcome to Day 17: AI for Sales & CRM
steps.body:
Sales is about relationships — but the admin, research, and follow-up that support those relationships can consume 60% of a salesperson's time. AI changes that.

Today you'll learn how to:
• Score and prioritize leads so you focus on the right prospects
• Automate personalized outreach at scale
• Manage your pipeline with AI-powered insights
• Get AI coaching on your sales calls
• Build follow-up sequences that convert without chasing people away

The best sales operators don't replace their intuition with AI. They use AI to eliminate the busywork so they can focus on what matters: conversations.

AI transforms the sales process at every stage:

🎯 LEAD SCORING — AI analyzes prospect data (industry, company size, engagement, behavior) and scores each lead so you know who to call first. No more guessing.

✉️ OUTREACH — AI researches each prospect and drafts personalized messages referencing their specific situation. No more generic templates.

📊 PIPELINE MANAGEMENT — AI tracks deal stages, identifies stalled opportunities, and recommends next actions. Your pipeline manages itself.

🎙️ CALL COACHING — AI transcribes and analyzes sales calls, identifying patterns: what questions win, where deals go sideways, which phrases close.

🔄 FOLLOW-UP SEQUENCES — AI structures multi-touch sequences with varied messages and optimal timing. Follow-up without the mental overhead.

steps.title: How Carlos Doubled His Pipeline with AI
steps.body:
Carlos is an enterprise sales rep managing 150+ prospects. Before AI, he spent 15 hours/week on research and manual follow-up, leaving only 15 hours for actual conversations.

His AI-powered sales system:

1. LEAD SCORING: Every Monday, AI scores his 150 leads by fit (demographics) + intent (engagement signals). Carlos focuses on the top 20.

2. OUTREACH: AI researches each top prospect — recent funding, org changes, content they shared — and drafts personalized initial emails.

3. PIPELINE DASHBOARD: AI monitors deal velocity, flags accounts that have gone quiet, suggests re-engagement timing.

4. CALL COACHING: After each call, AI transcribes and scores it: talk-to-listen ratio, objection handling, next steps captured. Carlos gets feedback he didn't have to ask for.

5. SEQUENCES: AI manages follow-up — if a prospect opens a case study email but doesn't reply, AI schedules a call follow-up 2 days later with a specific reference to what they opened.

RESULT: Carlos went from 15 hours admin + 15 hours calls → 5 hours admin + 25 hours calls. His pipeline doubled. His win rate went up because he was talking to the right people at the right time.

steps.question: What does AI-powered lead scoring actually score — and why does it matter?
options: It scores how much the lead is willing to pay — so you can adjust pricing
It scores each lead based on fit (how well they match your ideal customer) and intent (engagement signals) — so you prioritize prospects most likely to convert
It scores the salesperson's performance on each lead
It scores how difficult the lead will be to close
feedback: Exactly. AI lead scoring combines demographic fit (industry, company size, role) with behavioral intent (email opens, website visits, content downloads). A lead with high fit but low intent needs nurturing; one with high intent but low fit might not be worth the time. Score on both dimensions.

Not quite. Lead scoring evaluates both fit (is this person in my sweet spot?) and intent (are they showing buying signals?). The combination tells you who to prioritize. High fit + high intent = call now. Everything else gets sequenced appropriately.

steps.question: AI call coaching should only analyze what the salesperson said wrong — it's a tool for criticism.
feedback: Right. The best AI call coaching analyzes both sides of the conversation. It identifies not just what went wrong, but what went RIGHT — which questions sparked engagement, which responses overcame objections, which closing phrases worked. Positive reinforcement is as important as correction.

Not correct. Good call coaching is balanced. AI should highlight wins ('you handled that price objection perfectly') as well as growth areas ('you interrupted the prospect twice, which reduced rapport'). Use it as a development tool, not a criticism machine.

steps.question: In sales, a structured series of timed, multi-channel touchpoints designed to move a prospect toward a decision is called a follow-up ___.
feedback: Correct. A follow-up sequence is a planned series of touchpoints — email, call, LinkedIn message, value share — each with a specific purpose and timing. AI sequences adapt based on prospect behavior: opened but didn't reply? Different next message. Replied interested? Transition to next stage.

Good answer. The correct term is sequence (or cadence). It's a structured plan — not random follow-ups — where each touch has a purpose and timing, and the sequence adapts based on how the prospect responds.

steps.title: AI-Optimized Follow-Up Sequence
steps.body:
HERE'S A 6-TOUCH FOLLOW-UP SEQUENCE THAT CONVERTS:

Touch 1 (Day 1): Personalized email — reference trigger event, offer clear value, low-friction ask (15-min call?)

Touch 2 (Day 4): Value share — send a relevant case study or insight. 'Saw your industry is dealing with X — here's how a peer company solved it.' No hard sell.

Touch 3 (Day 8): LinkedIn connection request with a personalized note referencing your previous email.

Touch 4 (Day 12): Break-up email — letting them off the hook. 'I'll stop reaching out for now, but if solving [pain point] becomes a priority, let me know.'

Touch 5 (Day 20): Re-engagement — new insight or trigger. Industry news, product update, or relevant content.

Touch 6 (Day 30): Final reach-out — different angle. Shift your value proposition or approach. Then stop.

AI manages the timing, adapts the message based on opens/clicks/replies, and notifies you when a human touch is needed. Each touch has a different angle — never the same message repeated.

steps.question: Analyzing 500 past closed-won and closed-lost deals to identify the patterns, signals, and outreach timing that predict a win.
feedback: This is a high-value AI application. Humans can't manually analyze 500 deals and extract subtle patterns. AI can: it correlates timing, messaging, prospect behavior, and industry signals to surface what actually works. The output becomes your sales playbook.

Excellent fit. Pattern extraction from historical data is one of AI's core strengths. Feed it your CRM data and ask: 'What do our won deals have in common that lost deals don't?' The answers will reshape your approach.

steps.question: Negotiating the final terms of a multi-million dollar deal with a long-time client.
feedback: Right. Final-stage negotiations are high-stakes, nuanced, and deeply relational. AI can help by preparing data (comparable deals, pricing analysis, concessions history), but the human relationship and judgment are paramount in the negotiation itself.

Not a good fit for automation. AI can prepare you — pricing scenarios, comparable deals, risk analysis — but the actual negotiation is human work. Your relationship, judgment, and reading of the room are irreplaceable at this stage.

fields.label: Describe your ideal customer profile
fields.placeholder: e.g., SaaS companies with 50-200 employees, Series A/B, head of sales is decision-maker

fields.label: What are your pipeline stages?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Lead → Qualified → Demo → Proposal → Negotiation → Closed

fields.label: What signals indicate a lead is worth pursuing?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Opened 3+ emails, visited pricing page, downloaded case study, C-suite title

fields.label: What's your ideal follow-up pattern?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Email Day 1 → Social Day 3 → Call Day 7 → Value Share Day 14 → Breakup Day 21

steps.body:
SALES AI SYSTEM PROMPT

"You are an AI sales operations consultant analyzing my sales process.

My product/service: [DESCRIBE]
My target customer: [DESCRIBE]
My current process: [DESCRIBE FROM PROSPECTING TO CLOSE]

Analyze this and give me:
1. The 3 stages of my sales process that AI could most impact immediately
2. For each stage: what AI handles vs. what I handle
3. A simple lead scoring formula (fit + intent signals)
4. A follow-up sequence blueprint (6 touches, varied angles)
5. One AI coaching insight I could get from my past calls or emails

Be specific and actionable. I'm implementing this week."

Briefly describe your current sales process — even if you're not in sales, think about how you persuade or pitch ideas. Then explain which parts you'd automate with AI and which you'd keep as human work. Be specific about the AI/human split.

steps.question: You send a follow-up email to a prospect who hasn't replied. What's the best practice for the subject line?
options: Use the exact same subject line — consistency is key
Change the subject line to something different — signal that this is a new message with new value, not a repeat of the same email
Use 'RE:' — make it look like a reply to trick them into opening
Don't use a subject line — personal is better
feedback: Right. Changing the subject line signals that you're providing something new — not re-sending the same request. Each touch in your sequence should have a different angle, and a different subject line reinforces that. Avoid tricks like 'RE:' — they erode trust when discovered.

Good thought, but the best practice is a fresh subject line that indicates new value. Each follow-up should feel like a new outreach, not a reminder. And never use deceptive tactics like 'RE:' — trust matters more than an open rate.

steps.prompt:
Think about how sales and relationship-building work in your world.

• What's the most time-consuming part of your current outreach or persuasion process?
• If you could reclaim 10 hours/week by automating research and follow-up, what would you do with that time?
• What's one sales insight you suspect you'd learn if you analyzed your past conversations?

questions.label: What's your biggest sales pain point?
options: Finding and qualifying the right prospects
Closing — I get interest but not commitment

questions.label: What would you build first if you created an AI sales system today?
questions.placeholder: Describe the feature and how it would change your workflow...

steps.title: Day 17 Complete! 🎯
steps.body:
You now understand how AI transforms every stage of the sales process — from lead scoring and outreach to pipeline management, call coaching, and follow-up sequences.

Key takeaways:
• AI scores leads on fit + intent so you prioritize the right conversations
• Personalized outreach at scale beats generic templates every time
• Call coaching works both ways — what you did right AND wrong
• Follow-up sequences need varied angles, not repeated messages
• The AI handles the admin; you handle the relationship

Tomorrow: AI for Product Development — rapid prototyping, PRD generation, and user research automation.
